title: Licensing of Philippine Overseas Shipping Enterprise
content:
Section 44. Philippine Overseas Shipping Enterprises (POSE) which own/manage/operate Philippine flag ships trading overseas may apply for a special license subject to submission of: a) Articles of Incorporation; b) Certificate of Accreditation by MARINA; c) Certified true copy of certificate of ship registry issued by MARINA; d) Proof of payment of a non-refundable filing fee of PhP10,000.

Section 45. Upon approval, POSE shall pay a license fee of PhP50,000 and post a surety bond of PhP50,000 and escrow deposit of PhP200,000 with a commercial bank authorized by Bangko Sentral Ng Pilipinas.

Section 46. The Administration shall issue a special license within 24 hours from receipt of application if all requirements are met. Only complete applications are deemed filed.

Section 47. The special license is valid for 4 years from issuance, provided there is a valid MARINA certificate of accreditation, unless terminated, suspended, cancelled or revoked by the Secretary or authorized representative.

Section 48. For renewal, POSE shall submit a written application with: a) Certified copy of MARINA Certificate of Accreditation; b) Proof of renewed surety bond of PhP50,000; c) Certificate from bank that escrow deposit of PhP200,000 is intact; d) Payment of license fee of PhP50,000.

Section 49. POSE shall not collect any placement fees from their hired seafarers unless otherwise provided by law.
